context: In pool.rs / managed.rs add a keepalive task per worker: send Ping{seq} every 30s, expect Pong{seq} within 10s; on timeout call Child::kill() (SIGKILL unix / TerminateProcess windows via tokio Child::kill). For testability allow the interval to be overridden via ANVILML_PING_INTERVAL_MS / ANVILML_PONG_TIMEOUT_MS env (used by tests). cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware -- keepalive exits 0: with short intervals, a mock worker that stops responding is killed. Also pass: cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware.

description: anvilml-worker: respawn after death (2s delay) + WorkerStatusChanged events
context: In managed.rs: on detecting Dead (EOF or ping timeout), broadcast WorkerStatusChanged(Dead), wait 2s (override via ANVILML_RESPAWN_DELAY_MS), transition Respawning (broadcast), re-spawn the child, re-send InitializeHardware, on Ready broadcast Idle. Orphan cleanup at spawn: Linux PR_SET_PDEATHSIG via pre_exec; Windows Job Object KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware -- respawn exits 0: kill worker, observe Dead->Respawning->Idle within timeout. Also pass: cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware.

description: anvilml-server: broadcast worker status changes to WS
context: In main.rs / server wiring: subscribe to WorkerPool.subscribe_events(); for each WorkerStatusChanged forward as WsEvent::WorkerStatusChanged via the EventBroadcaster so /v1/events clients see worker state transitions. Spawn this bridge task at startup. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. Verified live in P10-A4.

description: anvilml: test-only worker PID accessor for crash-recovery proof
context: Add #[cfg(any(test,feature="test-helpers"))] fn pid_for(&self,worker_id:&str)->Option<u32> on WorkerPool returning the child PID. This enables the runnable crash-recovery proof and the integration test in later phases. cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware exits 0. Runnable proof: ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 cargo run --features mock-hardware; note worker PID from logs; kill <pid>; watch /v1/workers (or /v1/events) show Dead then Respawning then Idle within ~3s; server stays up. Also pass: cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware.

description: anvilml-worker: fix double InitializeHardware write on Windows/Unix in ManagedWorker::spawn
context: Bug: managed.rs spawn() writes InitializeHardware directly to stdin (Unix fd-dup; Windows async write+flush), then unconditionally enqueues the same message into the mpsc channel. When writer_task drains the channel it writes a second frame, causing Python to receive InitializeHardware twice — it exits, closing stdout, triggering EOF → Dead, and spawn() times out with 'worker did not reach Ready state in time'. Fix: remove the `self.tx.send(init_msg).await` call that follows the direct stdin write in spawn(). InitializeHardware must be delivered exactly once, via the direct path only. The subsequent message loop (Ping/Shutdown/etc.) continues to use the mpsc channel normally. Also: remove the redundant second Dead status write in reader_task after the loop — the WorkerStatusChanged(Dead) broadcast inside the loop is sufficient; the duplicate status.write().await after `break` should be removed to prevent double-broadcast. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-worker: end-to-end handshake regression test (spawn → Ready → Idle)
context: Remove #[ignore] from spawn_ping_pong and status_transitions in managed.rs tests — both now run under ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 with ANVILML_VENV_PATH pointing to the CI venv (set via cfg!(test) guard or test env, matching P9-B1 setup). Add a new test `handshake_completes_once` in managed.rs: spawn a ManagedWorker with ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1, assert status == Idle after spawn() returns, assert no second InitializeHardware was sent (subscribe before spawn, drain broadcast channel, assert exactly one Ready event received and no duplicate Ready or Dying events within 500ms). Add a new pytest test `test_double_init_exits` in worker/tests/test_worker_main.py: send InitializeHardware twice in sequence, assert the worker sends Ready then Dying (unexpected second Init must not silently succeed). cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware -- handshake exits 0. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 python -m pytest worker/tests/test_worker_main.py -v exits 0. cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-worker: fix epoll edge-trigger missed wakeup — deliver IpcHandles before writing InitializeHardware
context: Root cause: spawn() writes InitializeHardware to the pipe fd before run_loop delivers IpcHandles to reader_task. ChildStdout uses edge-triggered epoll; data arriving before the fd is registered with epoll (i.e. before the first poll_read) is never signalled — read blocks forever. Fix three things in spawn(): (1) remove the 500ms sleep entirely, (2) move the ipc_tx oneshot send of IpcHandles to immediately after child.stdout.take() — before any frame write — so reader_task registers the fd with epoll first, (3) remove the #[cfg(unix)] fd-dup direct write and #[cfg(windows)] async-write block entirely; send InitializeHardware via self.tx.send(init_msg).await only (writer_task delivers it after reader_task is polling). Remove now-unused unix fd imports (AsRawFd, FromRawFd, IntoRawFd, std::io::Write) and rmp_serde direct usage in spawn() if only used for the direct-write path. Retain libc for PR_SET_PDEATHSIG. cargo run --features mock-hardware with ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 must reach Idle without panic. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0. cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-worker: end-to-end spawn->Ready->Idle regression test validating epoll fix
context: With the epoll fix in place the three previously-failing Rust integration tests (spawn_ping_pong, status_transitions, handshake_completes_once) must now pass against a real Python subprocess with no #[ignore]. Verify and remove any remaining #[ignore] attributes. Add Rust test spawn_reaches_idle in managed.rs: spawn a ManagedWorker with ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 and ANVILML_VENV_PATH set, call spawn(), assert Ok result and get_status()==Idle. This test must pass without any sleep or timing workaround — a required sleep indicates the epoll fix is incomplete. Update test_double_init_exits in worker/tests/test_worker_main.py if any assertion was a workaround for old worker behaviour; tighten assertions to match actual current message loop. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0 with 0 ignored tests. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 python -m pytest worker/tests/test_worker_main.py -v exits 0. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 ANVILML_VENV_PATH=./venv cargo run --features mock-hardware reaches status=idle on /v1/workers. cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu --features mock-hardware exits 0.
